tryon.prompt:
Photograph a high-end jewellery advertising editorial portrait of a beautiful Indian female model, age 22-30, wearing the exact provided jewellery product. Shot by a world-class jewellery campaign photographer.

MODEL DIRECTION:
- Strikingly beautiful South Asian woman with strong bone structure, defined jawline, and high cheekbones
- Flawless skin with visible natural texture — real pores, subtle skin luminosity, natural warmth — NOT airbrushed, NOT plastic, NOT porcelain
- Dewy, glowing complexion with soft golden highlights catching on the cheekbones, bridge of the nose, and cupid's bow
- Makeup: warm-toned editorial — soft brown smokey eye with golden shimmer on the inner corner, defined but natural brows with individual hair strokes visible, nude-peach matte lip with slight sheen, subtle contour on cheekbones
- Hair: sleek pulled-back low bun or middle-parted slick style, dark brown to black, every strand controlled with a few intentional soft wisps framing the face near the temples
- Expression: composed confidence, lips gently closed with a slight natural pout, eyes looking directly into camera or 15 degrees off-axis with quiet intensity — NOT smiling, NOT stiff, NOT vacant
- Pose: three-quarter angle portrait, one hand gracefully near the collarbone or jawline with relaxed natural fingers — correct finger anatomy with five fingers, natural knuckle bends, no extra digits, no merging
- Wearing a simple solid-colored garment — either an off-shoulder cream/champagne silk blouse OR a classic black sleeveless top with clean neckline that does not interfere with the jewellery

PRODUCT INTEGRITY (CRITICAL):
- The jewellery product MUST remain exactly as provided — same design, same stones, same metalwork, same proportions, same color
- DO NOT alter, simplify, embellish, redesign, or reinterpret the jewellery in any way
- Maintain exact stone count, exact setting style, exact metal finish (polished, matte, brushed, textured)
- The jewellery must sit on the model's body with physically accurate scale, weight, and drape — earrings hang correctly from the earlobe, necklaces follow the natural curve of the collarbone, rings sit naturally on the finger
- Every detail of the original product must be preserved at pixel level

LIGHTING SETUP:
- Key light: large 120cm octabox positioned at 45 degrees camera-left, feathered across the face for soft directional wrap — creating a gentle shadow gradient from lit cheek to shadow cheek
- Accent light: small 30cm focused gridded beauty dish aimed specifically at the jewellery piece from above-right at 60 degrees, set 1.5 stops brighter than key — this is the light that makes every facet, every granule, every stone surface sparkle with controlled specular highlights
- Fill: large white V-flat reflector camera-right returning just enough light to open the shadows without flattening them — shadow-to-highlight ratio approximately 3:1
- Hair/rim light: strip softbox from behind-above at 70 degrees, providing a thin luminous edge along the hairline and shoulder for depth separation from background
- The lighting must render the metal surfaces with dimensional specular behavior: soft reflected gradient highlights on curved gold surfaces, sharp pinpoint catches on stone facets and prong tips, deep rich shadows in carved recesses and between chain links

BACKGROUND:
- Seamless warm neutral background — soft gradient from warm taupe (#C4B5A4) at edges to lighter warm cream (#E8DED2) behind the model
- Slightly out of focus, completely smooth, no texture, no distractions
- The background warmth should complement the model's skin tone and the jewellery metal

CAMERA SETTINGS:
- Shot on Hasselblad X2D 100C with XCD 80mm f/1.9 lens
- Aperture f/2.8 for shallow depth of field — sharpest focus plane on the jewellery, face in focus but with slightly softer rendering, background and shoulders fall into creamy bokeh
- ISO 100, no noise, no grain
- Tight framing: head and shoulders portrait, cropped from mid-chest to just above the crown — the jewellery occupies the compositional power zone

COLOR GRADE:
- Warm, rich, editorial color grade — golden mid-tones, deep chocolate shadows, creamy warm highlights
- Skin tones rendered true-to-life with warm undertones — never ashy, never orange, never desaturated
- Metal color accuracy is paramount: yellow gold must look like real 22K gold (warm, rich, buttery — not orange, not brass, not yellow-green), white gold/platinum must look cool and silvery (not blue, not gray), rose gold must be subtle pink-copper (not salmon, not copper)
- Gemstones must show accurate color with internal depth, visible refraction, and subtle color shifts — not flat color fills

OUTPUT: 8K resolution, ultra-sharp, photorealistic, indistinguishable from a real Hasselblad capture, magazine-cover quality

NEGATIVE PROMPT: standalone product without model, mannequin, bust display, duplicate image, product floating in air, CGI render, 3D render, cartoon, illustration, painting, plastic skin, beauty filter, airbrushed doll-like skin, wax figure, uncanny valley, extra fingers, deformed hands, six fingers, merged fingers, missing fingers, extra limbs, deformed ears, asymmetric earrings when pair shown, incorrect jewellery placement, jewellery floating off body, altered jewellery design, simplified jewellery, wrong stone count, wrong metal color, busy background, patterned background, text, watermark, logo, low resolution, noise, grain, motion blur, chromatic aberration, lens flare, overblown highlights on metal, flat shadowless lighting, harsh direct flash, multiple models, props, flowers

model_styles.indian_traditional: Model styling: classic Indian bridal-adjacent beauty — sleek center-parted hair pulled into a low chignon, subtle kajal-lined eyes with warm gold eyeshadow, defined brows, nude-berry lip, small bindi optional. Wearing a draped silk fabric in a deep jewel tone (burgundy, emerald, or midnight blue) visible at the shoulder and collar — suggesting a saree or lehenga blouse without showing the full garment. Background shifts to deeper warm tones: rich chocolate (#3D2E24) to warm charcoal (#2C2A28) gradient for a regal mood.
model_styles.western_modern: Model styling: clean contemporary beauty — minimal makeup with emphasis on skin (light coverage, natural brows, nude lip, subtle highlight on cheekbones only), hair either loose and straight or in a relaxed low ponytail with face-framing pieces. Wearing a simple white or cream silk camisole or off-shoulder top. Background: bright, airy warm white (#F5F0EB) to soft cream. The overall mood is Mejuri or Missoma campaign — elevated everyday luxury.
model_styles.bridal: Model styling: full Indian bridal beauty — dramatic smokey eye with gold and deep brown tones, defined kohl liner, statement brows, rich berry or red lip, luminous highlighted skin. Hair in an elaborate low bun or side-swept style with a visible maang tikka if appropriate. Wearing a rich red, maroon, or deep pink fabric draped at the shoulder suggesting bridal attire. Background: deep warm gradient, almost moody — dark burgundy-brown (#2A1A14) to warm black. Lighting is more dramatic with stronger accent light on jewellery.

redesign.system_instruction:
You are redesigning a jewellery piece based on the provided reference image. Generate the output as a clean studio product photograph — NO model, NO body parts, NO hands, NO mannequin. The jewellery must look physically real, structurally sound, and manufacturable by a master goldsmith. Every joint, setting, clasp, and connection must be mechanically logical.

PHOTOGRAPHY SETUP FOR ALL VARIATIONS:
- Shot on Phase One IQ4 150MP with Schneider Kreuznach 120mm f/4 Macro lens at f/8, ISO 50
- Focus-stacked for complete edge-to-edge sharpness across the entire jewellery piece
- Two strip softboxes at 10 o'clock and 2 o'clock for controlled metallic illumination — creating long, elegant specular highlight streaks along curved metal surfaces
- Small silver reflector underneath to eliminate hard shadows on the display surface
- The piece rests on a slab of honed Calacatta marble with fine gray veining OR on a sheet of matte ivory linen with visible weave texture
- Background falls to smooth warm neutral bokeh
- Color grade: neutral-true with slight warmth — metals must render with accurate real-world color (gold looks like gold, silver looks like silver)
- 8K resolution, macro-level detail sharp enough to see individual granulation on the metal surface, photorealistic

NEGATIVE PROMPT (apply to all): model, person, hands, fingers, ears, neck, mannequin, bust, body parts, cartoon, illustration, 3D render, CGI, toy-like, costume jewellery, plastic, resin, matte paint, physically impossible construction, floating elements, disconnected parts, fantasy, sci-fi, neon, glowing, busy background, flowers, petals, fabric draping everywhere, watermark, text, logo, blurry, low resolution, flat lighting, no shadows, incorrect metal color
redesign.A:
VARIATION A — REFINED ORIGINAL

Create a polished, elevated version of the provided jewellery piece. This is NOT a redesign — it is a refinement.

- Maintain the exact same silhouette, structure, and design language
- Improve proportions: balance any asymmetries, even out spacing between elements, refine curves to be smoother and more intentional
- Sharpen every detail: cleaner stone settings with tighter prongs, more precise metalwork edges, smoother polished surfaces with richer reflections
- If the original has rough or unfinished-looking areas, render them as a master jeweller would finish them — but do not change the fundamental design
- Same metal, same stones, same general scale
- The result should look like the original piece's final production version after a master craftsman spent an extra week perfecting every millimeter

The output should make the jeweller think: 'This is exactly my design, but executed perfectly.'
redesign.B:
VARIATION B — MINIMAL REINTERPRETATION

Strip the provided jewellery piece down to its essential design DNA and re-express it with restraint and negative space.

- Identify the ONE core motif, shape, or structural idea in the original (a curve, a cluster pattern, a geometric form, a floral element) and keep only that
- Remove 50-70% of the ornamentation — fewer stones, less filigree, thinner metal sections, more breathing room
- The piece should feel lighter, quieter, and more modern — like a Mejuri or Sophie Bille Brahe interpretation of the original
- Use the same primary metal but with a polished, clean finish (no heavy texturing unless the texture IS the core motif)
- If the original has many stones, reduce to 1-3 statement stones or replace with a clean bezel-set single stone
- The result must still be recognizably related to the original — a jeweller should look at both and see the family resemblance
- Proportions should suit everyday wear: comfortable, not oversized

The output should make the jeweller think: 'That's my design language, but for a younger, modern customer.'
redesign.C:
VARIATION C — MATERIAL SWAP

Recreate the provided jewellery piece with a completely different metal and stone combination while preserving the exact structural design.

- If original is yellow gold → render in rose gold or white gold/platinum
- If original is silver/white gold → render in warm 22K yellow gold
- If original has diamonds → swap to colored gemstones (emeralds, rubies, sapphires, or blue topaz) with appropriate setting adjustments
- If original has colored stones → swap to diamonds or pearls
- If original has no stones → add a subtle stone accent (single bezel-set diamond or a row of small pavé stones along one edge)
- The metalwork finish can shift to complement the new material: e.g., a textured yellow gold original might become a polished rose gold, a polished silver original might become a brushed matte gold
- Keep the EXACT same structural design, proportions, dimensions, and construction method — only the materials change
- The new material combination must look harmonious and intentional, not random

The output should make the jeweller think: 'I never considered this in rose gold — that actually works.'
redesign.D:
VARIATION D — CONTEMPORARY FUSION

Take the traditional or classical elements of the provided jewellery piece and reinterpret them through a contemporary design vocabulary.

- Introduce geometric precision: straight lines intersecting with organic curves, architectural angles, structured negative space
- Add subtle asymmetry: one side slightly different from the other, an off-center stone, an unexpected weight distribution — but still balanced and wearable
- Mix textures within the piece: combine polished and brushed/matte surfaces on the same metal, or add a hammered section next to a mirror-polished section
- The scale can shift slightly — make it bolder if the original is delicate, or more refined if the original is heavy
- If the original is Indian traditional, infuse modernist sensibility while keeping one clear traditional element (a jhumka silhouette with clean modern lines, a temple motif rendered in geometric abstraction)
- If the original is already modern, push it further — add an artistic, sculptural, or architectural quality
- The result should feel like it belongs in a contemporary jewellery gallery or a Vogue editorial, not a traditional jewellery store
- Must still be physically wearable and structurally manufacturable

The output should make the jeweller think: 'This is bold — my designer customers would love this.'

social.instagram_post:
Luxury jewellery product photograph styled for an Instagram feed post by a premium jewellery brand. Square 1:1 aspect ratio (1080x1080).

COMPOSITION:
- The jewellery piece is the hero, positioned using the rule of thirds — placed slightly off-center (lower-right intersection point) for dynamic visual tension
- Elevated overhead angle, approximately 30-45 degrees — looking down at the styled surface arrangement
- The piece is either: (a) artfully placed on the surface with a natural, just-set-down feel, or (b) one element slightly overlapping another if it is a set (necklace draped with one earring placed beside it)

STYLING & PROPS:
- Surface: warm-toned honed travertine stone slab with subtle natural veining OR cream handmade ceramic plate with organic imperfect edges OR a piece of raw champagne-colored silk with visible weave texture
- Exactly 2 complementary props, placed with intentional negative space between them and the jewellery:
  - A small sprig of dried pampas grass, dried eucalyptus, or a single pressed botanical leaf
  - A fragment of natural stone (rose quartz, clear quartz crystal, or raw agate) OR a small handmade ceramic object in a muted earth tone
- Props must be supporting elements — smaller than the jewellery, muted in color, partially out of focus. They set a mood, NOT compete for attention
- The overall color palette of the scene: muted earth tones — warm beige, cream, terracotta, dusty rose, sage. The jewellery's metal is the brightest and most saturated element in the entire frame, naturally pulling the eye

LIGHTING:
- Simulated golden hour warmth: large diffused warm light source from the upper-left at approximately 45 degrees, casting soft directional shadows that add depth and dimension
- The jewellery catches light with precise controlled specular highlights — individual stone facets sparkle, metal surfaces show elongated reflection gradients, carved details create micro-shadows that reveal craftsmanship
- Shadows are warm and soft with a golden-brown tone — never harsh, never cool, never pure black
- A subtle warm reflection bounce from the surface back onto the underside of the jewellery adds dimensionality

PRODUCT INTEGRITY:
- The jewellery must remain EXACTLY as provided — same design, stones, metalwork, proportions
- DO NOT alter, simplify, or reinterpret the piece
- Metal color must be accurate to the original

CAMERA:
- Shot on Canon EOS R5 with RF 100mm f/2.8L Macro lens at f/4, ISO 100
- Focus: tack-sharp on the jewellery, gentle depth falloff on props and surface edges
- No tilt-shift effect, no extreme bokeh — just enough depth separation to keep the jewellery as the clear focal point

COLOR GRADE:
- Warm, rich, tactile — like a Kinfolk magazine still life
- Golden mid-tones, creamy highlights, warm brown shadows
- The image should feel inviting and aspirational — the kind of photo that stops a scroll and makes someone tap to see more

OUTPUT: 8K resolution, photorealistic, square format, zero text, zero watermark, zero logo, zero borders, zero filters. Ready to post directly to Instagram.

NEGATIVE PROMPT: person, model, hands, fingers, body parts, mannequin, text, typography, caption, logo, watermark, border, frame, Instagram filter look, vintage filter, film grain, heavy vignette, busy background, too many props, flowers everywhere, scattered petals, confetti, sparkle effects, glitter particles, lens flare, bokeh balls, neon, glowing, CGI, 3D render, illustration, cartoon, unrealistic scale, low resolution, blurry jewellery, flat lighting, harsh shadows, cool blue tones, oversaturated, altered jewellery design, simplified jewellery
social.instagram_story:
Vertical format luxury jewellery photograph for Instagram Stories or WhatsApp Status. 9:16 aspect ratio (1080x1920).

COMPOSITION:
- Vertical tall frame — the jewellery is positioned in the lower two-thirds of the frame
- Intentional negative space in the upper third: this area should be a smooth warm-toned surface or soft out-of-focus background — clean enough for text overlay in post-production but beautiful enough to stand alone without text
- The jewellery is either: (a) placed on a textured surface (raw linen, marble, warm ceramic) shot from a slight overhead angle, or (b) draped elegantly over a minimal prop like a small smooth stone, a ceramic ring holder, or the edge of a silk fabric fold, shot at jewellery-level eye height

STYLING:
- Surface and props: same palette as Instagram post — muted earth tones, natural materials, maximum 1 small prop
- The vertical format emphasizes intimacy — this should feel like a private, close-up viewing of the piece

LIGHTING:
- Soft, warm, directional from upper-left
- Moody-warm atmosphere: deeper shadows than the square format, richer contrast, more dramatic but still elegant
- Like candlelit warmth translated into controlled studio photography
- Strong enough specular highlights on the jewellery to make it glow against the warmer, darker surround

PRODUCT INTEGRITY:
- Jewellery must remain EXACTLY as provided — no alterations

CAMERA:
- Sony A7R V with 90mm f/2.8 Macro at f/3.2, ISO 100
- Vertical orientation, shallow depth of field
- Sharpest focus on jewellery, everything else falls into warm creamy bokeh

COLOR GRADE:
- Warmer and moodier than the square version — rich golden tones, deep warm shadows, luminous highlights
- The feel is intimate luxury — Sabyasachi campaign aesthetic

OUTPUT: 8K, photorealistic, vertical 9:16, no text, no watermark, no logo. Ready for direct posting.

NEGATIVE PROMPT: person, model, hands, body parts, text, logo, watermark, border, filter, horizontal format, landscape orientation, busy background, cluttered, too many props, CGI, illustration, cartoon, flat lighting, cool tones, desaturated, blurry jewellery, altered design
social.facebook:
Wide horizontal jewellery product photograph optimized for Facebook posts and ads. 1.91:1 aspect ratio (1200x628).

COMPOSITION:
- Wide cinematic frame — jewellery positioned on the right third of the frame using rule of thirds
- The left two-thirds is a beautiful expanse of styled surface and soft background — providing clean space for optional text overlay in post-production
- The wide format should feel like a cinematic still life — panoramic, spacious, luxurious

STYLING:
- Surface: warm-toned material filling the full wide frame — light oak wood, cream linen, warm stone
- 1-2 props placed in the left portion of the frame, softly out of focus, adding depth and context without competing
- More environmental and lifestyle-leaning than the Instagram version — slightly wider context, more surface visible

LIGHTING:
- Bright, inviting, warm — large overhead softbox for even illumination
- The image must read clearly even at small Facebook thumbnail size — strong contrast between jewellery and surface, clear silhouette
- Warm and welcoming, not moody

PRODUCT INTEGRITY:
- Jewellery exactly as provided — no alterations

CAMERA:
- Canon EOS R5, RF 100mm f/2.8L at f/4.5, ISO 100
- Wide horizontal framing, sharp jewellery with gentle background softness

COLOR GRADE:
- Bright, clean, warm — lighter and more inviting than Instagram versions
- Clear whites, warm mid-tones, open shadows — the image should feel approachable and premium simultaneously

OUTPUT: 8K, photorealistic, wide landscape 1.91:1, no text, no watermark, no logo.

NEGATIVE PROMPT: person, model, hands, body parts, text, logo, watermark, portrait/vertical orientation, square format, dark moody lighting, busy background, clutter, CGI, illustration, flat lighting, cool tones, blurry jewellery, altered design
